title: Teek (Teek)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Teek}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:The_Teek.jpg]] |name=Teek |homeworld=[[Endor/Legends|Endor]]{{DB|character|teek|Teek}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Teek/Legends|Teek]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]'' |height=<1 [[Meter/Legends|meter]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Gray-white]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Peach |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You nasty little bug-lover!|Noa Briqualon curses Teek|}} '''Teek''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Teek/Legends|Teek]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and a [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] of the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. He was a playful, mischievous being, the only companion of a stranded [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] by the name of [[Noa Briqualon]], with whom he shared a [[Noa Briqualon's hut|cabin]] on Endor. Briqualon spent most of his [[Time/Legends|time]] attempting to repair his damaged [[Noa Briqualon's starship|starship]] and was generally hostile toward outsiders who stumbled upon his home. However, in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], a Human girl named [[Cindel Towani]] and her [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] companion, [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]], stumbled upon the house of Teek and Briqualon—Teek was quicker to warm up to the guests than Briqualon, but they both eventually formed a bond with the two children. Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after their first encounter, Towani was kidnapped by the [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Terak]], and Teek and his friend aided Warrick in her rescue. Teek proved vital to the recovery, using his superior speed to great effect, and after they rescued Towani, he helped [[Battle of Endor (Ewok–Sanyassan conflict)|fight]] Terak's [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] in the woods. He was ultimately left behind when Briqualon and Towani departed on the [[Starship/Legends|starship]], which the old Human had finally repaired, although he found a home with the Ewoks of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Teek was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Species/Legends|species]] of the [[Teek/Legends|same name]] [[Homeworld/Legends|from]] the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. He was a descedent of [[Teek|Teeks]] who arrived to the planet after the ship [[Starhunter Dhelba|''Starhunter Dhelba'']] crashlanded about [[800 BBY/Legends|800 BBY]].{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}} Teek eventually became the companion of [[Noa Briqualon]], an old [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] who had been stranded on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] ever since his [[Noa Briqualon's starship|starship]] crashed there. The two lived together in a small [[Noa Briqualon's hut|cabin]] near where Briqualon's [[Starship/Legends|starship]] lay for [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].{{Topps|set=2013 Topps Star Wars Galactic Files Series 2|cardname=Teek (Endor Companion)|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/93283/cid/6569583/2013-Topps-Star-Wars-Galactic-Files-Series-2-557-Teek}} ===Wicket and Cindel=== {{Quote|What's that?''"
"''Trouble!|Cindel Towani and Wicket on the arrival of Teek|}}[[File:Teek2.jpg|thumb|left|260px|Teek using his speed]] In 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' Teek stumbled upon two lost companions in the woods: a female Human [[Youngling/Legends|child]] named [[Cindel Towani]] and an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] named [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]]. The two had been captured by the [[Sanyassan Marauders|Sanyassan Marauder]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Terak]] but had managed to escape. However, the Sanyassans were determined to find Towani, thinking that she would help them unlock the secret of a piece of technology they had [[Thief/Legends|stolen]] during an [[Raid on Bright Tree Village|attack]] on her [[City/Legends|village]], an assault that [[Death/Legends|killed]] her [[Towani family|family]]. Teek tried to reach into Warrick's pocket but accidentally woke him up, prompting the Ewok to throw a stick at him; Teek dashed away. He then came back, laughing as he tossed [[Nut/Legends|nuts]]''[[The Ring, the Witch, and the Crystal: An Ewok Adventure]]'' at Warrick's head, which bothered him. Despite Warrick's apprehension, Towani did not let him hit Teek with his stick and decided to follow him, finding him amusing and hoping that he would lead them to [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]]. Teek mimed eating, indicating he understood, and led them to Briqualon's cabin, losing them multiple times due to his speed. Once there, the two children searched the house, playing with some of the items therein and amusing Teek. Mistakenly assuming the house to be unoccupied, they decided to stay, and Warrick began cooking food for the three of them. However, when Briqualon returned home, he was furious at his bemused friend for allowing strangers into the house and demanded that the children leave. ===Nothing but trouble=== {{Quote|Teek! Stay out of those pies and save some for tomorrow.|Noa Briqualon to Teek|}} [[File:Teek1lookattherest.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Teek meets Wicket W. Warrick and Cindel Towani.]] Once they had left, Briqualon further scolded Teek. He then noticed the food in the oven, offering some [[Porridge/Legends|porridge]] and [[Muffin/Legends|muffins]] to Teek. Towani and Warrick made camp nearby, and Teek brought them some of the muffins, disobeying Briqualon's instructions. He then sprinted back inside, twiddling his thumbs as though he had not left. The old scout eventually had a change of heart and allowed Towani and Warrick to stay for one [[Local day/Legends|night]] only, during which Towani had a [[Dream/Legends|nightmare]] that [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassans]] were coming for her. Despite her fragile mental state, Briqualon maintained that they would have to leave come morning, and a disappointed Teek accepted the decision. The children accepted Briqualon's terms and left in the morning, which he feigned being a delight and riddance of trouble for him. He offered Teek to choose a new bed from two he had fashioned, pretending as though he had always intended them for him. When the guests came back later with [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]] out of which they could make [[Pie/Legends|pies]], Briqualon reconsidered and allowed Warrick and Towani to stay with him and Teek as long as they proved themselves useful and helped out around the cabin. The next [[Standard day/Legends|day]], curious as to where Briqualon continued to disappear, the two guests followed him as he set off to work on the starship, despite Teek's best efforts to dissuade them. Briqualon discovered them, told them his story, and allowed them to stay in his house as long as they needed. However, in the early [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] of the morning, Towani was kidnapped by the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsister]]''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]'' [[Charal]], and after searching the surrounding [[Forest/Legends|forests]], Warrick and Briqualon left to rescue the young girl. Teek, though apprehensive at first, eventually sped off to assist them. ===Terak's Keep=== {{Quote|Teek!|Cindel Towani nearly jeopardizes the operation by calling attention to Teek's presence|}} [[File:TeekKeys-BFE.png|thumb|left|300px|Teek sneakily steals the keys.]] The trio arrived at [[Terak's Keep]], where Terak was holding Towani captive. When they reached the moat surrounding the large building, they opted not to swim across, as the [[Water/Legends|waters]] were infested with vicious creatures. Instead, they used Briqualon's [[Grappling hook/Legends|grappling hook]] to climb atop the great wall, where Teek and Warrick incapacitated the [[Unidentified eyepatched Sanyassan|patrolling guard]] and helped the larger Briqualon up onto the wall. The three of them searched the castle for Towani and discovered she was [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]], along with a group of Ewoks, in a nearby [[Prison/Legends|cell]]. Teek stole a guard's [[Clothing/Legends|uniform]] to get by the guards, which the trio used to disguise themselves. They reached the dungeon where Towani was being kept, though two additional Sanyassans, playing cards, sat close to Towani. Teek slipped one of the men's cards into the Sanyassan's sleeve; when the other player noticed, a fight ensued, leaving the two Sanyassans dead and Teek with the keys needed to free Towani and the Ewoks. With the Ewoks freed, Teek and the group of rescued prisoners began making fleeing the Keep, but Terak heard them escaping and sounded the alarms. Teek and the others eventually reached a dead end, but Briqualon used some explosives to blow a hole in the wall. Led by Terak, the Sanyassans soon reached the group, who was swinging across the moat on Briqualon's rope one by one. As Briqualon and Towani left, the young girl spotted a [[crystal oscillator]], the only remaining part Briqualon needed to get his ship running, which the old man took. Although the guards fired at them, Teek, Briqualon, Towani, and the Ewoks all escaped. However, Terak sent dozens of his men after the group. [[File:TeekAccost.jpg|thumb|right|265px|Teek attacks Terak.]] The escapees reached a forest, where Terak's [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], aided by Charal, tracked them. Briqualon led them to his ship, but it would take him several [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] to power up the vessel. Teek was sent to scout the area and discover how far away the Sanyassans were while the Ewoks assembled a powerful [[Catapult/Legends|catapult]] near the ship. Teek found Terak's men; however, he inadvertently led them back to Briqualon's ship, though he did fend off one Marauder by biting their finger. The Sanyassans clashed with the Ewoks in the forest, and although Teek did not join the effort against Terak's warriors, he dealt the warlord a blow when he rubbed two stones together. The resulting flames caused Terak's two massive [[Blurrg/Legends|blurrg]] beasts to retreat. Teek took control of one of the cannons on Briqualon's starship and fired back at the marauders. Through the efforts of Teek and his companions, Terak's warriors were driven back to the Keep, although the warlord himself remained to fight with Briqualon. Teek, perched high in a [[Tree/Legends|tree]], watched in horror as Terak stood over a helpless Briqualon with his [[Sword/Legends|sword]] raised. However, the [[Hair/Legends|furry]] creature intervened and saved his companion's life by jumping on Terak's head and unbalancing the Sanyassan, who flung Teek into nearby [[Plant/Legends|foliage]]. Through the efforts of Teek and Warrick, who inadvertently unlocked the dark magic in the warlord's ring, Terak was turned to stone. ===Goodbyes=== {{Quote|I love ya and I'm gonna miss ya.|Noa Briqualon says goodbye to Teek|}} [[File:Teek_and_wicket.jpg|thumb|left|270px|Teek and Warrick wave goodbye to Towani and Briqualon.]] With Terak defeated, Towani and Briqualon decided to leave Endor, although Teek opted to stay behind and live with the Ewoks. Briqualon said his emotional goodbyes to Teek, who was saddened that he would not see his companion again. Teek was also sad at the loss of Towani, although she vowed to return and see him and Warrick again. The starship successfully took off, and Teek, alongside Warrick, saw their friends leave the moon for good, waving all the while. He joined the Ewoks of Bright Tree Village and lived with them. Shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]][[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' the [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]] travel agency began offering [[Endor Express|tours]] of the forest moon. At some point, Teek boarded a [[StarSpeeder 3000]] and was featured in a safety video that showcased the vessel's under-the-seat storage.{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Star Tours (toy line)|packtype=Multipack|pack=STAR TOURS Boarding Party|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/StarToursBoardingParty.asp}} ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You're a sweet spirit.|Noa Briqualon|}} [[File:BrimleyHug.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Teek with his friend Noa Briqualon]] Teek had scruffy [[Color/Legends|gray-white]] fur and peach-colored skin, along with a pair of close-set brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and two oversized buck [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. Less than a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] tall, he often spoke through giggles and, due to his fast metabolism, he was extremely quick. Teek was friendly and hospitable toward others. When he first met Towani and Warrick, he led them to his home and offered them food and shelter, even though Briqualon was far less accommodating. When Teek worried for the two children, he sneaked muffins to Towani and Warrick, despite Briqualon's orders that he specifically not do so. Teek was mischievous though not harmful, and he spent much of his time laughing and giggling with others, something that Briqualon discouraged. He displayed a sense of mirth, such as when he threw nuts at Warrick, and he often ran off haphazardly without considering the consequences of his speedy gait. ==Skills and abilities== {{Quote|Got you!|Teek after hitting Wicket with a nut|}} Teek was extremely quick, and when at full speed, he often looked as if he was a mere blur. He used his speed as both a source of amusement and as a weapon of sorts—he once used his speed to tie a Sanyassan to a tree on Endor. He could also play a type of [[Flute/Legends|flute]], which he used with Towani, Briqualon, and Warrick when they played [[Music/Legends|music]] and danced together in Briqualon's house. He was fairly intelligent but not particularly strong, and he was not [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. ==Equipment== Teek carried a satchel. ==Behind the scenes== ===Production=== {{Quote|I did not even see it coming. One day they told me my call time was god awful early, like 6 am—for a sixteen year old that is early. I was to report to the creature shop. Next thing you know parents are there signing contracts with me. I had to join the SAG (Screen Actors Guild) as soon as possible. It was a bit crazy. You know, no one ever 'asked' me if I wanted to do the part. I was the tiniest of the bunch, always was and always will be.|Niki Botelho, on being cast as Teek|{{Blogspot|subdomain=starwarsinterviews1|url=2010/08/niki-botelho-teek-star-wars-battle-for.html|name=Star Wars Interviews|text=Niki Botelho interview}}}} [[File:TeekConcept.png|thumb|right|250px|Concept art of Teek]] Teek was created for the [[1985]] made-for-television film ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]''. The character was initially a puppet, but [[Wilford Brimley]]—who played the part of Noa Briqualon—vociferously objected to the idea of working with a puppet and often called on crewmember [[Niki Botelho]] to stand in his line of sight while he spoke with Teek. It was eventually decided that Teek would be played by an actor in a suit, and the diminutive, sixteen-year old Botelho was told to report to the film's creature shop to be fitted with a full body costume.{{BanthaCite|31|A Peak at Teek}} Botelho remembered not being asked if she wanted to expand her role and was rushed through the process, but she did not protest as the crew gave her cookies.{{WebCite|url=https://talkingbay94.libsyn.com/niki-botelho-bringing-teek-to-life-in-ewoks-the-battle-for-endor|text=Niki Botelho: Teek in Ewoks - The Battle for Endor|date=2020-09-23|archivedate=20230605073433}} The new costume was finished after four days of nonstop work. Teek's costume required molding, for which Botelho had straws placed in her noses to allow her to breathe, and it had two heads, one articulated and one not. The articulated head contained devices to make Teek's eyes and face move, and was operated by off-camera puppeteers. Botelho described the mask as feeling as though there was a machine on her face because of how many moving parts there were, requiring as many as six people total to control it.[[Bantha Tracks 30|''Bantha Tracks'' 30]] During the filming of one scene, Botelho fell down a hill and into a creek, and the weight of her costume nearly caused her to drown until crewmembers could rescue her and remove her mask. Scenes of Teek running were shot at a lower frame rate to make him look extremely fast, and special visual effects had to be used to achieve the effect when he ran in a shot with other characters.{{InsiderCite|Special Edition 2016|On Set With the Ewoks!}} Botelho altogether recalled the experience fondly. ===Other media=== {{Listen|filename=Teek.flac|title="Teek"|description=A theme for Teek appears in ''The Battle for Endor''}} [[File:Teek_feet.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Teek sits next to a man demonstrating the under-the-seat storage compartment at ''Star Tours''.]] In the [[Ewoks (soundtrack)|score]] for ''The Battle for Endor'', composer [[Peter Bernstein]] used a specific musical theme to represent Teek. The fast-paced motif is musically reminiscient of Teek's playful nature and his speedy running ability. The theme appears on the soundtrack LP in the track titled "Teek."[[Ewoks (soundtrack)|''Ewoks'' soundtrack]] Teek is additionally featured in a [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] safety video, portrayed by a puppet instead of Botelho. It was initially unclear if the character was in fact Teek or a different member of the Teek species, but a Teek action figure based on that appearance later confirmed the character's identity. Botelho was sent the figure and had the opportunity to meet its sculptor. The [[1995]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'', by [[Andy Mangels]], describes Briqualon's relationship to Teek as that of a "master."''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' However, as this is inconsistent with ''Battle for Endor'' itself and is not used in any other media, this article avoids using that language regarding their relationship. In the [[2013]] [[Canon|non-canon]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Tiny Death Star]]'', Teek was available among many other characters as a [[Bitizen]] that could be obtained from one of the game's [[W:c:Tiny-death-star:Salvage Droids|Salvage Missions]].''[[Star Wars: Tiny Death Star]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[The Ring, the Witch, and the Crystal: An Ewok Adventure]]'' appearances=== *''[[Star Wars: Tiny Death Star]]'' *[[Star Wars (2025) 7|''Star Wars'' (2025) 7]] {{C|Variant cover only}} ==Sources== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Teek}} Episode Guide | Star Wars: Skeleton Crew}} *{{InsiderCite|229|Era of the Ewoks}} }} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Teek (Person) |nl=Teek (Personage) }} [[Category:Endorians]]
